---
title: "Senior Domain Maps"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

# Make this QMD robust when Quarto executes from scripts/senior/.
for candidate in [Path.cwd(), *Path.cwd().parents]:
    if (candidate / "src").exists() and (candidate / "data").exists():
        sys.path.insert(0, str(candidate))
        break

import folium
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import yaml
from shapely import wkt

from src.map_utils import (
    add_categorical_point_layer,
    add_demographic_backdrop_layers,
    add_map_title,
    build_metric_bubble_map,
    find_project_dir,
    make_base_map,
)
```

```{python}
PROJECT_DIR = find_project_dir()
CLEAN = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean"
OUTPUT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "maps" / "senior"
METADATA_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "docs" / "metric_metadata.yml"

OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

senior_path = CLEAN / "senior.csv"
facilities_path = CLEAN / "senior_facilities.csv"
crashes_path = CLEAN / "senior_pedestrian_crashes.csv"
top_intersections_path = CLEAN / "senior_top_intersections.csv"

for path in [senior_path, facilities_path, crashes_path, top_intersections_path, METADATA_PATH]:
    if not path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(path)

metadata = yaml.safe_load(METADATA_PATH.read_text()) or {}

senior = pd.read_csv(senior_path, dtype={"GEOID": str}, low_memory=False)
senior["geometry"] = senior["geometry_wkt"].map(wkt.loads)
tracts = gpd.GeoDataFrame(senior, geometry="geometry", crs="EPSG:4326")

facilities = pd.read_csv(facilities_path, dtype={"GEOID": str}, low_memory=False)
crashes = pd.read_csv(crashes_path, dtype={"GEOID": str}, low_memory=False)
top_intersections = pd.read_csv(top_intersections_path, low_memory=False)

for frame in [facilities, crashes, top_intersections]:
    for column in ["latitude", "longitude"]:
        if column in frame.columns:
            frame[column] = pd.to_numeric(frame[column], errors="coerce")

numeric_columns = [
    "senior_population_65plus",
    "senior_population_share",
    "senior_lep_count_65plus",
    "senior_lep_rate_65plus",
    "other_languages_senior_lep_rate_65plus",
    "other_languages_senior_lep_count_65plus",
    "asian_pacific_senior_lep_rate_65plus",
    "asian_pacific_senior_lep_count_65plus",
    "other_indo_european_senior_lep_rate_65plus",
    "other_indo_european_senior_lep_count_65plus",
    "spanish_senior_lep_rate_65plus",
    "spanish_senior_lep_count_65plus",
    "asian_senior_poverty_rate_60plus",
    "black_senior_poverty_rate_60plus",
    "hispanic_senior_poverty_rate_60plus",
    "white_nh_senior_poverty_rate_60plus",
    "mltfam_walkup_rate",
    "total_mltfam_bldgs_3plus",
    "median_household_income",
    "age_65_plus_share",
    "age_65_plus_population",
    "white_non_hispanic_share",
    "black_non_hispanic_share",
    "asian_non_hispanic_share",
    "hispanic_share",
    "poverty_rate",
    "lep_household_share",
]
for column in numeric_columns:
    if column in tracts.columns:
        tracts[column] = pd.to_numeric(tracts[column], errors="coerce")
```

```{python}
demographic_keys = [
    key for key in [
        "median_household_income",
        "age_0_to_19_share",
        "age_20_to_64_share",
        "age_65_plus_share",
        "white_non_hispanic_share",
        "black_non_hispanic_share",
        "asian_non_hispanic_share",
        "hispanic_share",
        "poverty_rate",
        "lep_household_share",
    ]
    if key in metadata and key in tracts.columns and tracts[key].notna().any()
]

if not demographic_keys:
    raise KeyError(
        "No demographic backdrop metrics are available. Expected shared demographic columns in senior.csv and docs/metric_metadata.yml."
    )

demographic_specs = {key: metadata[key] for key in demographic_keys}

# map_utils.add_metric_layer() requires every demographic layer spec to have a dimension.
# Keep this local to Senior so we do not edit shared metadata used by other domains.
for key, spec in demographic_specs.items():
    spec.setdefault("dimension", spec.get("short_label", spec.get("label", key)))





service_site_spec = metadata["senior_service_sites"]
pedestrian_spec = metadata["senior_pedestrian_safety_points"]
top_intersection_spec = metadata["senior_pedestrian_top_intersections"]

service_category_colors = service_site_spec.get("category_colors", {})
pedestrian_category_colors = pedestrian_spec.get("category_colors", {})
top_intersection_category_colors = top_intersection_spec.get("category_colors", {})

for label, color_spec in [
    ("senior_service_sites.category_colors", service_category_colors),
    ("senior_pedestrian_safety_points.category_colors", pedestrian_category_colors),
    ("senior_pedestrian_top_intersections.category_colors", top_intersection_category_colors),
]:
    if not color_spec:
        raise KeyError(f"Missing {label} in docs/metric_metadata.yml")

```

## Senior service supply

```{python}
service_output = OUTPUT_DIR / "senior_service_supply.html"

facility_colors = service_category_colors

# Facility categories must match metadata exactly. Without this check,
# an unregistered category is silently removed by the .isin() filter below.
facility_types = sorted(
    facilities["facility_type"].dropna().astype(str).unique()
)
unregistered_facility_types = [
    facility_type
    for facility_type in facility_types
    if facility_type not in facility_colors
]
if unregistered_facility_types:
    raise KeyError(
        "Facility types missing from "
        "senior_service_sites.category_colors: "
        + ", ".join(unregistered_facility_types)
    )

service_map = make_base_map(tracts)
add_demographic_backdrop_layers(
    service_map,
    tracts,
    demographic_specs,
    none_option_label="No demographic background",
)

facilities_for_map = facilities[
    facilities["facility_type"].isin(facility_colors)
    & facilities["latitude"].notna()
    & facilities["longitude"].notna()
].copy()

norc_sites = facilities_for_map[
    facilities_for_map["facility_type"].eq("NORC")
].copy()

older_adult_centers = facilities_for_map[
    facilities_for_map["facility_type"].eq("Older Adult Center")
].copy()

add_categorical_point_layer(
    service_map,
    norc_sites,
    category_col="facility_type",
    category_colors={"NORC": facility_colors["NORC"]},
    name=f"NORCs (n={len(norc_sites)})",
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["facility_name", "facility_address"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Facility", "Address"],
    size=15,
    legend_bottom_offset=35,
)

add_categorical_point_layer(
    service_map,
    older_adult_centers,
    category_col="facility_type",
    category_colors={
        "Older Adult Center": facility_colors["Older Adult Center"]
    },
    name=f"Older Adult Centers (n={len(older_adult_centers)})",
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["facility_name", "facility_address"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Facility", "Address"],
    size=15,
    legend_bottom_offset=105,
)

add_map_title(
    service_map,
    "CB3 Seniors: Senior service supply",
    "DFTA-funded Older Adult Centers and NORCs over selectable demographic backdrops.",
)
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(service_map)
service_map.save(service_output)

print(f"Saved: {service_output.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```

## Senior pedestrian safety

```{python}
pedestrian_output = OUTPUT_DIR / "senior_pedestrian_safety.html"

pedestrian_map = make_base_map(tracts)
add_demographic_backdrop_layers(
    pedestrian_map,
    tracts,
    demographic_specs,
    none_option_label="No demographic background",
)

if crashes.empty:
    print("Warning: no senior pedestrian crash points available.")
else:
    incident_colors = {
        key: value
        for key, value in pedestrian_category_colors.items()
        if key == "Senior pedestrian injury/incident"
    }
    fatality_colors = {
        key: value
        for key, value in pedestrian_category_colors.items()
        if key == "Senior pedestrian fatality"
    }

    incidents = crashes[
        crashes["incident_type"].eq("Senior pedestrian injury/incident")
        & crashes["latitude"].notna()
        & crashes["longitude"].notna()
    ].copy()
    fatalities = crashes[
        crashes["incident_type"].eq("Senior pedestrian fatality")
        & crashes["latitude"].notna()
        & crashes["longitude"].notna()
    ].copy()

    if not incidents.empty:
        add_categorical_point_layer(
            pedestrian_map,
            incidents,
            category_col="incident_type",
            category_colors=incident_colors,
            name=f"Senior pedestrian incidents (n={len(incidents)})",
            lat_col="latitude",
            lon_col="longitude",
            tooltip_fields=["crash_date", "intersection", "PERSON_AGE", "PERSON_INJURY"],
            tooltip_aliases=["Crash date", "Location", "Age", "Injury"],
            size=10,
            legend_bottom_offset=35,
        )

    if not fatalities.empty:
        add_categorical_point_layer(
            pedestrian_map,
            fatalities,
            category_col="incident_type",
            category_colors=fatality_colors,
            name=f"Senior pedestrian fatalities (n={len(fatalities)})",
            lat_col="latitude",
            lon_col="longitude",
            tooltip_fields=["crash_date", "intersection", "PERSON_AGE", "PERSON_INJURY"],
            tooltip_aliases=["Crash date", "Location", "Age", "Injury"],
            size=20,
            legend_bottom_offset=135,
        )

if not top_intersections.empty:
    top_intersections["point_type"] = "Top senior pedestrian crash intersection"
    top_colors = top_intersection_category_colors
    add_categorical_point_layer(
        pedestrian_map,
        top_intersections.dropna(subset=["latitude", "longitude"]).copy(),
        category_col="point_type",
        category_colors=top_colors,
        name="Top 5 senior pedestrian crash intersections",
        lat_col="latitude",
        lon_col="longitude",
        tooltip_fields=["label"],
        tooltip_aliases=["Intersection"],
        size=22,
        legend_bottom_offset=235,
    )

fatality_count = int(crashes["fatality"].sum()) if "fatality" in crashes.columns and not crashes.empty else 0
incident_count = len(crashes)
add_map_title(
    pedestrian_map,
    "CB3 Seniors: Senior pedestrian safety",
    f"{incident_count} senior pedestrian incidents in 2024–2025; fatalities: {fatality_count}.",
)
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(pedestrian_map)
pedestrian_map.save(pedestrian_output)

print(f"Saved: {pedestrian_output.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```

## Senior poverty by race

```{python}
poverty_metrics = {
    "asian_senior_poverty_rate_60plus": {
        "title": "CB3 Seniors: Asian senior poverty",
    },
    "black_senior_poverty_rate_60plus": {
        "title": "CB3 Seniors: Black senior poverty",
    },
    "hispanic_senior_poverty_rate_60plus": {
        "title": "CB3 Seniors: Hispanic senior poverty",
    },
    "white_nh_senior_poverty_rate_60plus": {
        "title": "CB3 Seniors: White non-Hispanic senior poverty",
    },
}


for metric, settings in poverty_metrics.items():
    if metric not in tracts.columns or tracts[metric].notna().sum() == 0:
        print(f"Warning: {metric} is unavailable.")
        continue

    if metric not in metadata:
        print(f"Warning: {metric} is missing from docs/metric_metadata.yml.")
        continue

    output_path = OUTPUT_DIR / metadata[metric]["filename"]

    build_metric_bubble_map(
        tracts=tracts,
        metric_specs={metric: metadata[metric]},
        demographic_specs=demographic_specs,
        metric=metric,
        title=settings["title"],
        subtitle=(
            f"Bubbles: {metadata[metric]['label']}. "
            "Background: selectable demographic layer."
        ),
        output_path=output_path,
    )

    print(f"Saved: {output_path.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```

## Senior LEP

```{python}
lep_language_metrics = {
    "spanish_senior_lep_rate_65plus": {
        "title": "CB3 Seniors: Senior LEP — Spanish",
    },
    "other_indo_european_senior_lep_rate_65plus": {
        "title": "CB3 Seniors: Senior LEP — Other Indo-European languages",
    },
    "asian_pacific_senior_lep_rate_65plus": {
        "title": "CB3 Seniors: Senior LEP — Asian and Pacific Island languages",
    },
    "other_languages_senior_lep_rate_65plus": {
        "title": "CB3 Seniors: Senior LEP — Other languages",
    },
}

for metric, settings in lep_language_metrics.items():
    if metric not in tracts.columns or tracts[metric].notna().sum() == 0:
        print(f"Warning: {metric} is unavailable.")
        continue

    if metric not in metadata:
        print(f"Warning: {metric} is missing from docs/metric_metadata.yml.")
        continue

    output_path = OUTPUT_DIR / metadata[metric]["filename"]

    build_metric_bubble_map(
        tracts=tracts,
        metric_specs={metric: metadata[metric]},
        demographic_specs=demographic_specs,
        metric=metric,
        title=settings["title"],
        subtitle=(
            f"Bubbles: {metadata[metric]['label']}. "
            "Background: selectable demographic layer."
        ),
        output_path=output_path,
    )

    print(f"Saved: {output_path.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```

## Senior housing access

```{python}
housing_metric = "mltfam_walkup_rate"

if housing_metric not in tracts.columns or tracts[housing_metric].notna().sum() == 0:
    print(
        "Warning: walk-up rate is unavailable. "
        "Add MapPLUTO/HPD elevator raw files or Kailey's tract-rate fallback with walk-up fields."
    )
else:
    housing_output = OUTPUT_DIR / "senior_housing_walkup_access.html"
    build_metric_bubble_map(
        tracts=tracts,
        metric_specs={housing_metric: metadata[housing_metric]},
        demographic_specs=demographic_specs,
        metric=housing_metric,
        title="CB3 Seniors: Senior housing access",
        subtitle=(
            "Bubbles: estimated share of 3+ floor multifamily buildings without elevator access. "
            "Background: selectable demographic layer. Senior-targeted affordable unit locations "
            "require additional Furman SHD / HPD Local Law 44 inputs."
        ),
        output_path=housing_output,
    )
    print(f"Saved: {housing_output.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```

## Validation summary

```{python}
outputs = sorted(OUTPUT_DIR.glob("*.html"))
summary = pd.DataFrame(
    [{"html_file": path.name, "size_bytes": path.stat().st_size} for path in outputs]
)
display(summary)

expected = {
    "senior_service_supply.html",
    "senior_pedestrian_safety.html",
    "senior_poverty_asian.html",
    "senior_poverty_black.html",
    "senior_poverty_hispanic.html",
    "senior_poverty_white_non_hispanic.html",
    "senior_lep_spanish.html",
    "senior_lep_other_indo_european.html",
    "senior_lep_asian_pacific.html",
    "senior_lep_other_languages.html",
    "senior_housing_walkup_access.html",
}
created = {path.name for path in outputs}
missing = sorted(expected - created)

if missing:
    print("Warning: expected maps not created:")
    for name in missing:
        print(f"  - {name}")
else:
    print("All expected Senior maps created.")

print("SharePoint map files:")
for path in outputs:
    print(f"  {path.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```
